success_surface: Cell-Level Data Transformation (Vertical to Horizontal with Unique+Sort)
effective_behaviors: Conduct preliminary structure exploration before implementing final solution
Skip header rows when reading data columns (start from row 2)
Handle numeric type conversions gracefully (float to int when appropriate)
Use set-based deduplication followed by sorting for unique ordered extraction
Verify written output by reading back cells after modification
Save to new output file path rather than modifying source in-place
Apply minimal formatting (e.g., borders) only when it aids visibility without disrupting layout
evidence: Step 1: Explored column headers and sample data from Column H before creating solution code
Read loop started from row_idx = 2, correctly skipping the 'Number List' header
Used float-to-int conversion logic: 'if not str(val).endswith(".0"): val = int(val)'
Applied sorted(list(set(data_in_h))) for unique + sorted extraction
Included verification loop at end to read back Q2:V2 values before completion
Output path '/mnt/data/outputs/.../output.xlsx' kept separate from input path
Added thin border style to result cells for visual clarity

memory_items.title: Pre-Coding Structure Discovery
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure (sheet names, column headers, data samples) before generating transformation code
memory_items.generalization: For any spreadsheet task, first programmatically enumerate available sheets and key column layouts to verify target ranges match expectations

memory_items.title: Header-Aware Data Extraction
memory_items.lesson: When extracting data from a column, start from row 2 to skip the header row unless instructed otherwise
memory_items.generalization: Default to row 2 for data reads; only include row 1 if header is explicitly part of the dataset to process

memory_items.title: Numeric Type Normalization
memory_items.lesson: Convert float values that represent integers (ending in .0) to int type for cleaner output
memory_items.generalization: When processing mixed numeric data, normalize float/int representation based on decimal significance to maintain consistent data types

memory_items.title: Write-Then-Verify Pattern
memory_items.lesson: After writing to cells, immediately read back those cells to confirm values were written correctly
memory_items.generalization: For all critical write operations, add a verification read step before saving or returning success

memory_items.title: Source File Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Save transformed workbooks to a new output path rather than overwriting the original file
memory_items.generalization: Maintain file integrity by never modifying source workbooks in-place; always use a distinct output filename

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Structure First**: Before writing transformation code, explore sheet names, column headers, and sample data rows to confirm targets.
- **Skip Headers By Default**: Start data extraction from row 2 unless the header is explicitly part of the processing scope.
- **Normalize Numeric Types**: Convert floats representing whole numbers to integers for cleaner output (check decimal significance).
- **Verify Writes**: After writing values to cells, read them back to confirm correctness before finalizing.
- **Preserve Source Files**: Always write to a new output path; never modify the source workbook directly.
